title: Qualcomm: Highest ROE Chipmaker Stock To Buy
text: Qualcomm's high return on equity is driven by effective financial leverage, favorable net margins, and enhanced operational efficiency. The company's net margins are expected to improve modestly, rising to 31.3% by 2027, while its asset turnover ratio is anticipated to progress due to its fabless business model. Despite a slightly lower price target of $231.24, the company maintains a Strong Buy rating with a 110% upside, as it is expected to maintain stable margins and a lower 5-year forward growth rate of 7%.

title: Why TSMC, Texas Instruments, and Qualcomm Stocks Popped

title: ZeroNox Appoints Karna Patel as Chief Financial Officer
text: PORTERVILLE, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Zero Nox Inc. (“ZeroNox” or the “Company”), a leading provider of sustainable, off-highway vehicle electrification, today announced the appointment of Karna Patel as Chief Financial Officer (“CFO”). Mr. Patel served most recently as Senior Director of Finance at Qualcomm (Nasdaq: QCOM) and CFO of the Compute and Gaming business unit. His responsibilities included leading planning, budgeting, forecasting, pricing, accounting, reporting, and analysis efforts.

title: Qualcomm to acquire Israel's auto-chip maker Autotalks for $350M
text: Qualcomm Inc said that it would acquire Israel's Autotalks Ltd, a maker of chips used in crash-prevention technology in cars, as the US wireless-chip giant looks to expand its automotive-related business. This puts Autotalks among other impressive Israeli start-up exits, including Waze's $1.1 billion acquisition by Google in 2013, Mobilieye's $15.3 billion acquisition by Intel in 2017, and Mellanox's $6.9 billion acquisition by Nvidia in 2019, according to The Jerusalem Post.

title: Qualcomm boosting auto chip holdings with Autotalks acquisition
text: Qualcomm Inc. QCOM said Monday it agreed to buy Israel-based auto chip maker Autotalks to strengthen its portfolio of Snapdragon chips for automakers. While Qualcomm did not provide an amount, the company is reportedly paying $350 million to $400 million for the company, according to TechCrunch, citing unnamed sources.

title: Qualcomm's Expected 2023 Rally Fizzles, but Is the Stock Still a Good Value?
text: Qualcomm's latest quarter was mostly negative, and forward guidance indicates a delay in a sales rebound. Despite issues arising from the economy and oversupply in the wake of the pandemic, Qualcomm remains highly profitable.

title: Qualcomm to acquire Israeli auto-chip maker Autotalks

title: Qualcomm stock falls after guidance disappoints and weak sales to Apple for its iPhone
text: Qualcomm Inc stock fell after the wireless-chip giant reported second-quarter earnings for the fiscal year 2023 that were in line with Wall Street estimates but offered a light forecast. Investors also digested a surprise weakness in sales to Apple Inc (NASDAQ:AAPL) for its iPhone and margin lows not seen in nearly three years, according to MarketWatch.

title: Why Qualcomm Stock Is Falling Today
text: Qualcomm said smartphone demand will be slower than previously expected. The company issued third-quarter guidance that spooked investors.

title: Qualcomm stock falls as backed up Apple iPhone inventory contributes to weak outlook
text: Qualcomm Inc. shares dropped Thursday after Wall Street digested a surprise weakness in sales to Apple Inc. for its iPhone and margin lows not seen in nearly three years.

title: Is It Time For Qualcomm To Start Playing Catchup?
text: Qualcomm Inc NASDAQ: QCOM released its Q2 financial results after the bell on Wednesday, with investors watching closely to see if they could match the optimistic beats of many of their tech peers. For Qualcomm, in particular, an upside beat and upbeat forward guidance were very much needed, as their shares have been trading sideways since October.

title: Qualcomm amplifies chip gloom with 'sobering report'
text: Qualcomm Inc's shares sank nearly 8% premarket on Thursday after the chip designer signalled it would take longer for its crucial smartphone market to rebound from a post-pandemic slump.

title: Qualcomm (QCOM) Misses Q2 Earnings Estimates

title: A Tough Smartphone Market Will Weigh On Qualcomm's Q2 Results
text: QCOM is expected to publish its Q2 FY'23 results on May 3, reporting on a quarter that is likely to see the company's revenue contract meaningfully year-over-year, due to a slowdown in smartphone and tablet sales.
